title: On the Peg
body: One Bral shall be redeemable, in perpetuity, for one (1) standard 330ml beer — lager, pilsner, or nearest equivalent. No floating rate. No revaluation. No exceptions, excepting Article VII.

title: On the Metal
body: Bral coins shall be struck exclusively from aluminum sourced from beverage cans emptied by, or in the presence of, a licensed Minter. No virgin ore. No smelted scrap of uncertain provenance.

title: On the Reserves
body: The Treasury shall maintain a strategic reserve of no fewer than four hundred (400) unmelted cans at all times, stored in the Foundry vault and audited biannually by a non-Minter witness.

title: On the Mint
body: Striking shall occur on Saturdays, weather permitting. Remote mints are permissible under Branch Charter. All struck coins receive a hand-stamped Mint Mark (B·) upon issue.

title: On Redemption
body: Holders may redeem Bral for beer at any licensed Redemption Window. The Window opens Saturdays 19:00–23:00. Closure is at the Governor's sole discretion.

title: On Counterfeits
body: Any coin struck from non-licensed aluminum, or bearing a forged Mint Mark, shall be melted down and re-issued. The perpetrator forfeits one (1) round.

title: On Exceptions
body: The Governor, by unanimous consent of the Mint, may issue a Commemorative Strike at a temporary rate of 1 : 2 beers. Such issuance shall not exceed twenty-four (24) coins per calendar year.
